item.title: War Rebound Sends Muni Bonds to Best April Since 2014
item.description: The municipal-bond market roared back in April to deliver its best performance for that month in over a decade, as investors found their footing following a stretch of war-fueled volatility.

item.title: Nuclear AI Startup Fermi Promised Land and Ample Power. But It Couldn't Sign a Single Client
item.description: Now the ex-CEO is waging a battle for Fermi’s future and its vision of atomic-powered data centers in the Texas panhandle.

item.title: ‘Final Fantasy XIV’ Faces Key Test as Thousands of Fans Gather
item.description: The longrunning online game has a lot of work to do to recover from the disappointing ‘Dawntrail’ expansion

item.title: Meta Acquires Robotics AI Company to Help Build Humanoid Technology
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has acquired Assured Robot Intelligence, a startup developing artificial intelligence models for robots, as part of a major initiative to build humanoid technology.

item.title: Surprise Colombian Rate Decision Helps Avoid Volatility, Central Banker Says
item.description: Colombia’s surprise decision to halt interest rate increases this week was intended to prevent upheaval, but may come at a cost, according to central bank co-director Mauricio Villamizar.

item.title: Drugmaker Seaport Climbs 17% After $255 Million Upsized US IPO
item.description: Seaport Therapeutics Inc., shares jumped 17% in the firm’s trading debut, after the company raised nearly $255 million in an upsized initial public offering.

item.title: Russian Charged in Oil and Gas Facility Hacks Pleads Guilty
item.description: A Russian hacker who helped to breach critical oil and gas facilities in the US, Ukraine and other countries pleaded guilty to charges that carry as many as 27 years in prison.

item.title: World’s Fourth Largest Gold Miner’s IPO Is Said to Be Paused
item.description: Work on the initial public offering of Navoi Mining & Metallurgical Co. has been paused, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Uzbek government weighs the right moment to list one of the world’s largest gold miners.

item.title: How the Internet Has Changed in Just the Last Week
item.description: Get ready for more meters

item.title: Roblox to Challenge Unity, Unreal Engines With New AI Software
item.description: Roblox Corp. is launching a new product that will position its popular video-game creation software to better compete with Unity Software Inc. and Epic Games, whose engines are used by most big-budget game-makers.

item.title: EQT Picks Banks to Explore Options for WS Audiology
item.description: EQT AB has picked investment banks to advise on options for hearing aid maker WS Audiology, including a possible initial public offering in Copenhagen, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Venetian Resort Las Vegas Seeks $2.35 Billion to Refinance Debt
item.description: The iconic Venetian Resort Las Vegas is seeking to raise $2.35 billion from debt investors to refinance its capital structure, joining a flurry of high-yield deals as investor appetite for riskier debt rebounds.

item.title: Leo Pharma Is Said to Add More Banks to Copenhagen IPO Lineup
item.description: Leo Pharma AS has picked more banks to help it prepare for a possible initial public offering in Copenhagen, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: BP Reviews UK North Sea Assets as New CEO Eyes Disposals
item.description: BP Plc is considering potentially exiting part or all of its operations in the UK North Sea, people familiar with the matter said, as the oil giant works to strip assets and pay down debt.

item.title: Copper Costs Turn Negative for Southern, Vale as Gold and Silver Byproducts Soar
item.description: For copper miners Southern Copper Corp. and Vale SA, surging prices of byproducts like gold and silver are creating an unusual situation: The cost of producing the red metal has turned negative.

item.title: Peru Inflation Unexpectedly Speeds Up to Fastest Pace Since 2023
item.description: Inflation in Peru’s capital unexpectedly accelerated for a sixth month in April to hit the fastest pace since late 2023.

item.title: SEC Semiannual Reporting Proposal Clears White House Review
item.description: A Securities and Exchange Commission plan to let public companies cut quarterly disclosures to twice a year moved closer to reality after passing a White House review.

item.title: Versant’s SportsEngine to Sell to Genstar’s PlayMetrics
item.description: Versant Media Group Inc., the owner of the Golf Channel and E!, has agreed to sell the youth-sports app SportsEngine to PlayMetrics, a competitor backed by Genstar Capital, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Manufacturing Holds Up as Costs Gauge Hits Four-Year High
item.description: This year’s US manufacturing expansion extended into April even as the Iran war drove input prices sharply higher.

item.title: Iran Grain Flows Slump as US Blockade Cuts Access to Key Port
item.description: Crop shipments to Iran through the Strait of Hormuz have fallen more than 40% from March, with a prolonged US blockade threatening to worsen the country’s already high food inflation.

item.title: Colombia Central Bank Shocks Traders and Fuels Steepener Bets
item.description: An unexpected pause in Colombia’s interest-rate hikes is threatening to upend local markets, with skepticism over the central bank’s credibility building weeks before a pivotal presidential election.

item.title: Caterpillar’s Biggest Bear Folds as AI Power Demand Soars
item.description: Caterpillar Inc.’s biggest Wall Street bear has thrown in the towel after watching the stock more than double on the back of surging demand for the company’s power generation equipment.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Set for Oil Windfall After Hormuz Boosts Prices
item.description: The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is creating an economic split among oil exporters in the Persian Gulf, with Saudi Arabia and Oman set for a windfall and others including the United Arab Emirates seeing a drop in petrodollar income.

item.title: Thiel’s Founders Fund Raises $6 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Haul
item.description: Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund has raised $6 billion for a new fund to invest in later-stage companies, according to people familiar with the matter, marking the firm’s largest haul ever.

item.title: US Natural Gas Futures Extend Gains on Lower Production, LNG Dip
item.description: US natural gas futures rose, adding to Thursday’s 12-cent jump, as producers rein in output and feedgas to the Cameron LNG terminal in Louisiana remains lower.

item.title: Tether’s Gold Hoard Nears $20 Billion as Buying Spree Continues
item.description: Tether Holdings SA bought more than six tons of gold for its reserves in the first three months of the year, extending a buying streak that’s made it the largest known holder of bullion in the world outside of banks and nation states.

item.title: ECB’s Makhlouf Says Upside Risks to Inflation Have Intensified
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf said yesterday’s initial assessment of the euro-area economy’s health, combined with weaker consumer and business confidence, suggests “near-term headwinds” to growth.

item.title: Exxon CEO Says Price Caps, Export Bans Will Worsen Energy Woes
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. Chief Executive Officer Darren Woods warned that national bans on exporting fuel hurt the global market and restrict needed supplies.

item.title: Bank of England’s Sole Dissenter Makes Case for Quick Rate Hikes
item.description: The Bank of England should be raising interest rates with greater urgency after entering the Iran war with slowing progress in its battle to bring down inflation, according to its chief economist.

item.title: Trump Family Crypto Project Quietly Sold as Holders Got Stuck
item.description: The pitch was straightforward: Invest in the cryptocurrency venture of Donald Trump and his family, back the industry’s most powerful ally at the peak of their influence, and share the spoils.
